# awscc_route53globalresolver_global_resolver (Resource)

Resource schema for AWS::Route53GlobalResolver::GlobalResolver



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String)
- `regions` (List of String)

### Optional

- `client_token` (String)
- `description` (String)
- `ip_address_type` (String)
- `observability_region` (String)
- `tags` (Attributes List) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--tags))

### Read-Only

- `arn` (String)
- `created_at` (String)
- `dns_name` (String)
- `global_resolver_id` (String)
- `i_pv_4_addresses` (List of String)
- `i_pv_6_addresses` (List of String)
- `id` (String) Uniquely identifies the resource.
- `status` (String)
- `updated_at` (String)

<a id="nestedatt--tags"></a>
### Nested Schema for `tags`

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.12.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `identity` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = awscc_route53globalresolver_global_resolver.example
  identity = {
    global_resolver_id = "global_resolver_id"
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
### Identity Schema

#### Required

- `global_resolver_id` (String)

#### Optional

- `account_id` (String) AWS Account where this resource is managed
- `region` (String) Region where this resource is managed

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = awscc_route53globalresolver_global_resolver.example
  id = "global_resolver_id"
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
$ terraform import awscc_route53globalresolver_global_resolver.example "global_resolver_id"
```
